CREATE TABLE server_status (id INT PRIMARY KEY, status VARCHAR(255), uptime VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO server_status (id, status, uptime) VALUES (1, 'active', '13 hour(s), 12 minute(s));
INSERT INTO server_status (id, status, uptime) VALUES (2, 'inactive', '0 hour(s), 0 minute(s));
SELECT uptime() AS current_uptime, (SELECT status FROM server_status WHERE id = 1) AS server_status FROM server_status;
SELECT server_status.status, uptime() FROM server_status JOIN (SELECT uptime() AS current_uptime) AS uptime_info ON server_status.id = 1;
CREATE TABLE logs (log_id INT PRIMARY KEY, log_detail VARCHAR(255), log_time TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO logs (log_id, log_detail, log_time) VALUES (1, 'Server started', CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
SELECT server_status.uptime, logs.log_detail FROM server_status LEFT JOIN logs ON server_status.id = 1;
SELECT uptime() WHERE EXISTS (SELECT * FROM server_status WHERE id = 2 AND status = 'inactive');
CREATE VIEW active_servers AS SELECT * FROM server_status WHERE status = 'active';
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM active_servers WHERE uptime() LIKE '%hour(s)%';
SELECT uptime() AS uptime_info FROM server_status WHERE uptime() IS NOT NULL;
